items.title: World Cup 2026 : England to face Argentina in tense semi-final
items.description: Switzerland meanwhile is eliminated after losing to Argentina 3-1. Mac Allister gave the reigning champions the lead only ten minutes into the game, heading the ball past Gregor Kobel off a Leo Messi cross. Switzerland gained in momentum, culminating in an equalizer from Dan Ndoye. But that goal was quickly overshadowed after Embolo was handed a second yellow for diving and was sent off. That obviously made the challenge even greater for Switzerland, but la Nati defended valiantly one man down, dragging Argentina into extra time. In the end though, Julian Alvarez scored a stunning goal curled in from outside the box. As Switzerland pushed for a second leveller, they were caught on the counter, and suffered a third and final goal at the hands of an unconvincing Argentinian side...

And it's causing all sorts of headaches for governments worldwide, particularly in Europe, as they make choices about what to invest in over the immediate future.

items.title: Benjamin Netanyahu pays tribute to Lindsey Graham, "a great friend of Israel"

"In our recent meeting, I said, 'Lindsey is a great friend of Israel and a cherished friend of mine. We have no better friend than Lindsey.' Lindsey understood that the security of Israel and America are inseparable," he said in a statement released by his office. "He devoted his life to defending America, strengthening our alliance and standing up for the free world. Israel has lost one of its greatest friends." items.description: Graham often advised Trump on foreign affairs, particularly on matters pertaining to Israel, Ukraine and Iran. He was a frequent visitor at the White House. Shirli Sitbon tells us more.

items.title: Te Deum: Archbishop urges end to 'insults and cruelty' as Milei watches on
items.description: In what some saw as a pointed rebuke to President Javier Milei, Archbishop of Buenos Aires Jorge García Cuerva calls on leaders to “stop disparaging others, regardless of their political party,” as he urges greater “care for the poor, the sick and the disabled” in annual ‘Te Deum’ speech. Leer más

items.title: Lagos task force impounds 309 motorcycles
items.description: The Lagos State taskforce said 309 commercial motorcycles were impounded during operation along the Lagos-Badagry Expressway. The post Lagos task force impounds 309 motorcycles appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: US and its Latin American partners urge 'peaceful' transition in Colombia
items.description: In addition to Washington, signatories included Argentina, Bolivia, Chile, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guyana, Honduras, Panama, Paraguay, and Trinidad and Tobago. Leer más

items.title: DR Congo says Ebola outbreak yet to peak as virus spreads to new areas
items.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DR Congo) remains in a "very active" phase and has yet to peak, health authorities said Thursday

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: South Africa World Cup midfielder Adams dies aged 25
items.description: South Africa and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams dies at the age of 25, just weeks after featuring for his country at the World Cup.

items.title: Palestinian aid worker who organized World Cup screenings killed in Israeli strike
items.description: Palestinians are mourning Mohammed al-Wahidi, a beloved aid worker in Gaza. He was killed by as Israeli airstrike while en route to a World Cup screening which he organized.

items.title: Argentina’s political parties look for ways to avoid extremes
items.description: With next year’s election set to offer a referendum on President Javier Milei’s reformist agenda, some influential business leaders and opposition figures are discussing the possibility that violent shifts of policy are coming to an end – regardless of who prevails in the 2027 vote. Leer más

items.title: World Cup culture wars and the legend of Lionel Messi
items.description: World Cups don’t occur in a vacuum. Rather, they are an integral part of the construction of cultural subjectivity across the globe given the prevalence and capillarity of football. Argentina’s central role in global football and its obsessed population add complexity and intensity. Leer más

items.title: Messi World Cup magic masks familiar penalty frailty
items.description: Argentina's captain has a 77% overall career conversion rate from 150 attempts from the spot, bang on the global average. What stands out is such a frailty for a player who is anything but average. Leer más

items.title: West Asia war updates: Iran warns it will not be bound by deal with U.S. if violations continue
items.description: Iran's supreme leader ​Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei said ‌that ​avenging his ⁠slain predecessor and father was "the demand of ‌the nation" and "must certainly" take ‌place, according ‌to ⁠a written ⁠message released on his Telegram account

items.title: Sinner beats Zverev to defend Wimbledon men’s singles tennis title
items.description: Italy's Jannik Sinner beats Germany's Alexander Zverev 6-7 (7-9) 7-6 (7-2) 6-3 6-4 to claim his fifth Grand Slam.

items.title: Argentina cling to Messi in bid to break Europe’s World Cup grip
items.description: Reigning champions are the only non-European side left in the tournament and must overcome Switzerland to keep Lionel Messi’s dream alive. Leer más

items.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader vows revenge  for father’s killing
Iran expands attacks on Gulf states after intense US strikes
items.description: Iran reported strikes Sunday evening on two of its southern islands while Kuwait, where Tehran has repeatedly targeted US installations, said border posts and an offshore oil platform had been attacked. The as-yet unclaimed strikes came hours after Tehran and WashingtonStrait of Hormuz, offering contradicting statements as to whether the vital waterway was open to traffic.

items.title: US Democrat Ro Khanna says he was detained by armed Israeli settlers
items.description: Khanna, 49, was visiting the occupied West Bank when he was held for 90 minutes, he says.

items.title: At least 27 killed in Bangkok bar fire
items.description: Firefighters arrived at the scene in the Chatuchak district to find patrons running through blasting flames to escape.

items.title: Lindsey Graham, hardline backer of US-Israeli wars, dies aged 71
items.description: US senator Lindsey Graham, a key ally of Israel and one of Washington’s most prominent foreign policy hawks, has died.

items.title: Government intervenes as shipping shocks expose container vulnerability
items.description: The first outcome of the ₹10,000 crore container manufacturing scheme announced in Union Budget 2026-27 came on July 3, when an India-made EXIM container built by DCM Shriram Group was unveiled at Dadri for Maersk, which has placed a follow-on order for another 1,000 containers

items.title: Scaloni on Messi taking penalties, VAR controversies and Switzerland clash
items.description: Argentina's national team coach discusses if Lionel Messi will keep taking penalties, accusations of referring bias in his side's favour and the pride he feels representing his nation. Leer más

items.title: Hungary public media reform begins with black screen apology
items.description: People tuning in to public service media in Hungary on Tuesday were greeted by a black screen and an apology. Peter Magyar has begun dismantling one of Viktor Orban's most potent tools of power.

items.title: Is the Strait of Hormuz still Iran's trump card?
items.description: Iran's latest attacks show it can easily disrupt shipping, energy markets and draw in Gulf neighbors. DW asks how much leverage Tehran really has over Washington and if its high-risk strategy could backfire.

items.title: US rapper Pitbull sets bald cap world record at London show
items.description: US rapper Pitbull has set the first Guinness World Record for the largest gathering of people wearing bald caps.

items.title: U.S., U.K., and 12 other nations reaffirm 2016 ruling invalidating China’s claims in South China Sea
items.description: A joint statement issued by the nations said they rejected “destabilising” actions in the disputed waters that threaten regional peace

items.title: Israel releases video of huge detonations in Lebanon
items.description: The Israeli military has released video showing huge explosions and detonations in villages in southern Lebanon.

items.title: Sinner beats Zverev to retain Wimbledon title
items.description: Jannik Sinner retained his Wimbledon title by beating Alexander Zverev in Sunday's men's final. The Italian claims his fifth Grand Slam crown and extends his dominance over the German to 10 straight victories.

items.title: Germany news: Neuschwanstein killer fights extradition to US
items.description: A man who murdered a woman at Neuschwanstein Castle says he fears execution in the US. Germany has been hit by another heat wave, though no records are likely to be broken this time, forecasters say.

items.title: How undercover agents and informants helped the FBI unravel Lawrence Bishnoi’s gang | Explained
items.description: A years-long U.S. investigation culminated in charges against 37 people linked to alleged transnational crime syndicates. Here’s how the FBI pieced together the case

items.title: Mauritania frees lawmakers convicted over discrimination claims
items.description: Mauritania's President Mohamed Ould Cheikh El Ghazouani has pardoned two opposition lawmakers who were sentenced to four years in prison after accusing him of racial discrimination against Black Mauritanians and the descendants of enslaved people.

items.title: Argentina to repay US$4 billion, defying critics who doubted tack
items.description: Argentina is set to make a major payment on its dollar bonds this week, pulling off a feat few investors thought possible. Leer más

items.title: Malian army retakes key northern town of Anefis after week-long battle
items.description: Mali's army has regained control of the key northern town of Anefis after fighting against rebel militants for nearly a week, Tuareg separatists and a military source said Friday.

items.title: U.S. delegation in Lebanon to discuss Israel ‘pilot zone’ withdrawal: official
items.description: Under a framework agreement reached on June 26, Israel will gradually withdraw from areas of southern Lebanon where it has deployed troops to fight Hezbollah, the Iran-backed Shia movement

items.title: When London stank to high heaven
items.description: In 1858, London sweltered through an unbearable heat wave. The River Thames reeked so badly it left people gasping for air. "The Great Stink" went down in history — and ultimately gave London a modern sewer system.

items.title: Granit Xhaka tells Swiss fans to 'keep dreaming' ahead of Argentina World Cup clash
items.description: Skipper said Switzerland's "overarching aim" was to beat the defending champions and reach the semi-finals for the first time. Leer más

items.title: Mayor of Berlin says he won't run for reelection
items.description: Berlin Mayor Kai Wegner withdrew from the reelection race this fall. His decision to play tennis during the power outage in January was among missteps that has dented his reputation.

items.title: France opens terror probe after gun found in Jewish suburb
items.description: Police said they found a "military-grade weapon" during the search of a suspicious car parked in a Paris neighborhood with a large Jewish population.

items.title: IMF confirms 3.5% growth forecast for Argentina in 2026
items.description: Multilateral lender’s chief Kristalina Georgieva to visit Buenos Aires this month; International Monetary Fund sees Argentina’s economy expanding 4% in 2027. Leer más

items.title: Kenya: MSF trains health workers to be sent to Ebola-hit DR Congo
items.description: An Ebola training facility set up by Doctors Without Borders in Kenya is a chance for health workers to prepare for one of the most complex emergencies they are ever likely to face.

items.title: Ukraine's Bidnyi 'outraged' by Russia's Olympic return
items.description: In exclusive comments to DW, Ukraine's sports minister, Matviy Bidnyi, said he is outraged by the IOC opening the door to Russia's return to the Olympics
